children.name: Picoides
children.description: Picoides — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Picoides arcticus
children.description: The black-backed woodpecker, also known as the Arctic three-toed woodpecker, is a medium-sized woodpecker inhabiting the forests of North America.

children.name: Picoides dorsalis
children.description: The American three-toed woodpecker is a medium-sized woodpecker, which is native to North America.

children.name: Picoides fumigatus
children.description: The smoky-brown woodpecker is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in Argentina, Belize, Bolivia, Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Peru, and Venezuela.

children.name: Picoides funebris
children.description: The Eurasian three-toed woodpecker is a medium-sized woodpecker that is found from northern Europe across northern Asia to Japan.

children.name: Picoides nanus
children.description: The brown-capped pygmy woodpecker or Indian pygmy woodpecker is a species of very small woodpecker found in Nepal, India and Sri Lanka. Some taxonomic authorities continue to place this species in the genus Dendrocopos or Picoides. This species is non-migratory and often spotted in pairs or small family groups.

children.name: Picoides tridactylus
children.description: The Eurasian three-toed woodpecker is a medium-sized woodpecker that is found from northern Europe across northern Asia to Japan.

children.name: Geocolaptes
children.description: Geocolaptes — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Geocolaptes olivaceus
children.description: The ground woodpecker is one of only three ground-dwelling woodpeckers in the world. It inhabits rather barren, steep, boulder-strewn slopes in relatively cool hilly and mountainous areas of South Africa, Lesotho and Eswatini and has yet to be recorded outside of Southern Africa. It is found in a broad swath running from southwest to northeast, from the Cape Peninsula and Namaqualand to Mpumalanga.

children.name: Nesoctites
children.description: Nesoctites — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Nesoctites micromegas
children.description: The Antillean piculet is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is endemic to the Caribbean island of Hispaniola that is shared by the Dominican Republic and Haiti.

children.name: Dryocopus
children.description: Dryocopus — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Dryocopus martius
children.description: The black woodpecker is a large woodpecker that lives in mature forests across the northern Palearctic. It is the sole representative of its genus in that region, and its range is expanding. The black woodpecker is easily the largest woodpecker species in Europe as well as in the portion of Asia where it lives and is one of the largest species worldwide.

children.name: Dryocopus schulzii
children.description: The black-bodied woodpecker is a Near Threatened species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, and Paraguay.

children.name: Dendrocopos
children.description: Dendrocopos — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Dendrocopos leucotos x major
children.description: Dendrocopos leucotos x major — a species in the genus Dendrocopos, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Dendrocopos major
children.description: The great spotted woodpecker is a medium-sized woodpecker with pied black and white plumage and a red patch on the lower belly. Males and young birds also have red markings on the neck or head. This species is found across the Palearctic including parts of North Africa.

children.name: Dendrocopos syriacus
children.description: The  Syrian woodpecker  is a member of the woodpecker family, the Picidae.

children.name: Chrysocolaptes
children.description: Chrysocolaptes — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Meiglyptes
children.description: Meiglyptes — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Melanerpes
children.description: Melanerpes — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Melanerpes carolinus
children.description: The red-bellied woodpecker is a medium-sized woodpecker of the family Picidae. It breeds mainly in the eastern United States, ranging as far south as Florida and as far north as Canada. Though it has a vivid orange-red crown and nape it is not to be confused with the red-headed woodpecker, a separate species of woodpecker in the same genus with an entirely red head and neck that sports a solid black back and white belly.

children.name: Melanerpes formicivorus
children.description: The acorn woodpecker is a medium-sized woodpecker with a length of around 20 cm (8 in), and an average weight of 85 g (3.0 oz). It is found across Central America, as well as North into the western United States and South into parts of Colombia.

children.name: Melanerpes shawi
children.description: {{speciesbox | image = |image_caption = | fossil_range = | genus = Melanerpes | species = shawi | authority = Campbell & Bochenski, 2021 }}.

children.name: Jynx spec
children.description: Jynx spec — a species in the genus Jynx, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Colaptes

children.name: Mulleripicus

children.name: Veniliornis
children.description: Veniliornis — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Veniliornis affinis
children.description: The red-stained woodpecker is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela.

children.name: Veniliornis callonotus
children.description: The scarlet-backed woodpecker is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in Colombia, Ecuador and Peru.

children.name: Veniliornis cassini
children.description: The golden-collared woodpecker is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in Brazil, the Guianas, and Venezuela.

children.name: Veniliornis chocoensis
children.description: The Choco woodpecker is a near threatened species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Veniliornis frontalis
children.description: The dot-fronted woodpecker is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in Argentina and Bolivia.

children.name: Veniliornis kirkii
children.description: The red-rumped woodpecker is a species of bird in the subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found from Costa Rica south to Peru and east to Brazil, Guyana, and Trinidad and Tobago.

children.name: Veniliornis maculifrons
children.description: The yellow-eared woodpecker is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is endemic to eastern Brazil.

children.name: Veniliornis nigriceps
children.description: The bar-bellied woodpecker is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.

children.name: Veniliornis passerinus
children.description: The little woodpecker is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in every mainland South American country except Chile, Suriname, and Uruguay.

children.name: Veniliornis sanguineus
children.description: The blood-coloured woodpecker is a species of bird in subfamily Picinae of the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in Guyana and Suriname.

children.name: Dendropicos
children.description: Dendropicos — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Picus
children.description: Picus — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Picus benghalensis
children.description: Picus benghalensis — a species in the genus Picus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Picus canus
children.description: The grey-headed woodpecker, also known as the grey-faced woodpecker, is a Eurasian member of the woodpecker family, Picidae. Along with the more commonly found European green woodpecker and the Iberian green woodpecker, it is one of three closely related species found in Europe. Its distribution also stretches across large parts of the central and Eastern Palaearctic, all the way to the Pacific Ocean and south to the Himalaya and the Malay Peninsula.

children.name: Picus gaudryi
children.description: Picus gaudryi — a species in the genus Picus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Picus hirundinaceus
children.description: Picus hirundinaceus — a species in the genus Picus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Picus javanensis
children.description: Picus javanensis — a species in the genus Picus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Picus loricatus
children.description: Picus loricatus — a species in the genus Picus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Picus macrocephalus
children.description: Picus macrocephalus — a species in the genus Picus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Picus malaccensis
children.description: Picus malaccensis — a species in the genus Picus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Picus peregrinabundus
children.description: {{Speciesbox |fossil_range = Late Miocene, |taxon = Picus peregrinabundus |authority = Umanskaja, 1981 }}.

children.name: Picus semirostris

children.name: Picus senegallensis
children.description: Picus senegallensis — a species in the genus Picus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Picus spec
children.description: Picus spec — a species in the genus Picus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.

children.name: Picus viridis
children.description: The European green woodpecker, also known as the yaffle and sometimes called a nickle, is a large green woodpecker with a bright red crown and a black moustache. Males have a red centre to the moustache stripe which is absent in females. It is resident across much of Europe and the western Palearctic but in Spain and Portugal it is replaced by the similar Iberian green woodpecker.

children.name: Dendrocoptes
children.description: Dendrocoptes — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Dendrocoptes medius
children.description: The middle spotted woodpecker is a mainly European woodpecker belonging to the genus Dendrocoptes.

children.name: Chloropicus
children.description: Chloropicus — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Chloropicus abyssinicus
children.description: The Abyssinian woodpecker, also known as the golden-backed woodpecker or the golden-mantled woodpecker, is a species of bird in the woodpecker family, Picidae. It is native to Africa, where it occurs in Eritrea and Ethiopia. It appears to be a close relative of the cardinal woodpecker Dendropicos fuscescens.

children.name: Chloropicus elachus
children.description: The little grey woodpecker, also known as the Sahelian woodpecker, is a species of bird in the family Picidae. It is found in Cameroon, Chad, Gambia, Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, and Sudan. This species is described as somewhat rare, but it has a very large range and the population appears to be steady, so the International Union for Conservation of Nature has rated its conservation status as being of "least concern".

children.name: Chloropicus elliotii
children.description: Elliot's woodpecker is a species of bird in the family Picidae. It has an extremely wide range and is described as an uncommon species. However, no particular threats to this species have been established, and the International Union for Conservation of Nature has rated its conservation status as being of "least concern".

children.name: Chloropicus fuscescens
children.description: The cardinal woodpecker is a widespread and common resident breeder in much of sub-Saharan Africa. It occurs in a wide range of habitats, ranging from dense forest to thorn bush. It is fairly vocal and is easily identified by its call notes.

children.name: Chloropicus gabonensis
children.description: The Gabon woodpecker is a species of small woodpecker in the family Picidae which occurs in western central Africa.

children.name: Chloropicus griseocephalus
children.description: The olive woodpecker is a species of bird in the woodpecker family Picidae. It is in the Dendropicos genus, which comprises small woodpeckers native to Sub-Saharan Africa. There are three described subspecies, all inhabiting various regions of southeast Africa; the red-bellied olive woodpecker, the montane olive woodpecker, and the southern olive woodpecker.

children.name: Chloropicus lugubris
children.description: The melancholy woodpecker is a species of woodpecker. It is found in West Africa from Sierra Leone east to Nigeria, living in forests, forest edges, clearings and woodlands. It is sometimes considered to be a subspecies of the Gabon woodpecker.

children.name: Chloropicus obsoletus
children.description: The brown-backed woodpecker is a species of bird in the woodpecker family Picidae. It is found in a belt across the savannah region of sub-Saharan Africa from Senegal in the west to Ethiopia, Kenya and Tanzania in the east. It is generally uncommon, but has a very large range and the population appears to be steady, so the International Union for Conservation of Nature has rated its conservation status as being of "least concern".

children.name: Chloropicus poecilolaemus
children.description: The speckle-breasted woodpecker is a species of bird in the family Picidae, which is native to sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Chloropicus stierlingi
children.description: Stierling's woodpecker is a species of bird in the family Picidae. It is native to Malawi, Mozambique, and Tanzania where its natural habitat is tropical dry forests in the Eastern miombo woodlands ecoregion. It is threatened by habitat destruction.

children.name: Yungipicus
children.description: Yungipicus — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Leiopicus
children.description: Leiopicus — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Pardipicus
children.description: Pardipicus — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Homalopus
children.description: Homalopus — a genus of woodpeckers in the family Picidae.

children.name: Homalopus picoides
children.description: Homalopus picoides — a species in the genus Homalopus, family Picidae. Also known as woodpeckers.
